meta.title: Ece Dalpolat — AI Engineer & Data Scientist
meta.description: Portfolio of Ece Dalpolat, AI Engineer & Data Scientist

hero.bio: I'm a production AI engineer: I take data to models, models to secure services, and services into live products. Not demos — measurable, documented, scalable systems. Currently pursuing an MSc at Işık University.

about.label: Background
about.title: About Me
about.p1: I'm an AI & LLM engineer focused on production systems — I take data to models, models to secure services, and services into live products. Not demos: measurable, documented, scalable systems. I hold an MSc in Information Technologies (Işık University) and a BSc in Software Engineering (Kırklareli University).
about.p2: I build and ship end to end. My flagship is agentic-rag, a self-verifying autonomous RAG runtime; I published a job-hunt agent and my own LLM-as-judge evaluation framework (openeval) to PyPI — and I use openeval to measure my other systems. My recurring signature across everything: security, observability, and grounding LLM output in real evidence.
about.p3: At Qkare (2025–2026) I own core pieces of a multi-tenant enterprise analytics stack: the dbt transformation layer (100+ models, native PostgreSQL RLS, AI precompute marts), LangChain / LangGraph agents, and FastAPI microservices — all observable and deployed to production.

e1.desc: Primary engineer on Qkare's B2B AI platform: dbt transformation layer (109+ models, RLS, AI precompute marts), behavioral analytics agents (LangChain ReAct, tenant-safe SQL), and advisor microservices (KPI & competency selection). Built the agent API gateway, corporate chatbot, and supporting CI/Docker infrastructure.

e2.desc: Conduct weekly lectures on data science and machine learning. Guide participants through hands-on projects, evaluate submissions, and support the learning process.

e3.desc: Assisted faculty development and taught software lab courses. Supervised students building software projects with a focus on data science and deep learning.

e4.desc: Collected and processed data across honey value chains. Developed and optimized deep learning models using Python, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn, and integrated them with external systems through APIs.

e5.desc: Developed automation tests using Selenium and TestComplete. Conducted manual testing to keep software stable, functional, and aligned with user requirements.

e6.desc: Built scalable software solutions using Java, Eclipse/IntelliJ IDEA, and SQL. Contributed to software development work in a national research environment.

e7.desc: Developed applications using ASP.NET MVC, C#, SQL Server, and Visual Studio. Strengthened team communication and collaboration skills in active software projects.

e1.desc: Specialisation in Data Science and Machine Learning. Thesis focuses on using machine learning to automate processes in beekeeping and honey extraction.

e2.desc: Foundation in software development, algorithms, database systems, and software testing methodologies.

contact.label: Get in touch
contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: I'm open to new opportunities, collaborations, and interesting conversations about AI, data engineering, and ML. Feel free to reach out through any of the channels below.

agenticrag.title: Agentic Knowledge Runtime
agenticrag.desc: Autonomous RAG over documents, SQL, and logs with a built-in self-verification loop. A LangGraph cyclic graph (Researcher → Analyst → Auditor) where the Auditor returns a structured boolean and loops back for re-retrieval whenever an answer isn't faithful — with dual brakes: a business-rule max-revisions limit and an infra recursion limit. Co-lead — I own the agent graph, RAG core, security, and observability layers; built with a partner, not solo.
agenticrag.highlightTitle: My contribution — agent graph, retrieval, and safety
agenticrag.highlightDesc: As co-lead I own the agent graph, the RAG core, and the security and observability layers. This was built with a partner, not solo — my focus was the self-verification loop, hybrid retrieval, the read-only SQL boundary, and making the whole runtime traceable and faithful.

c1.title: Multi-agent self-verification graph
c1.desc: LangGraph Researcher/Analyst/Auditor loop; the Auditor uses structured output (a real bool, not parsed prose). Message history accumulates while retrieval results are overwritten, so every revision re-runs against fresh evidence.

c2.title: Hybrid retrieval + rerank
c2.desc: Qdrant dense + sparse (RRF) top-20 → cross-encoder rerank → top-5; a heading-aware chunker that carries a breadcrumb (A > B > C) on every chunk, plus a separate row-oriented serializer for tables.

c3.title: MCP read-only SQL boundary
c3.desc: A DB-level sentinel_ro role that physically cannot write, plus an application-level ensure_read_only() guard: comment stripping, literal blanking, single-statement SELECT/WITH only, and a denied-keyword set.

c4.title: Product surface & security
c4.desc: JWT RS256 auth, a server-injected tenant_id (the model can never choose its own tenant), typed citation DTOs, a /chat endpoint with thread continuity, and a Streamlit operator console.

c5.title: Observability & CI
c5.desc: Fixed a real bug where instrumentation ran before the tracer provider existed, so spans never reached Phoenix; CI publishes to GHCR with Trivy scanning, and a Ragas faithfulness score below 0.85 fails the build.

jobhunt.title: Job-Hunt Agent
jobhunt.sub: Personal · Jul 2026 · Agentic job search + CV tailoring
jobhunt.desc: An end-to-end agent that extracts a master profile from your CVs, scrapes and ranks job postings by a fit score, generates a tailored CV and cover letter per posting through a cv-tailor subagent, and sends applications only with human approval. Built on deepagents / LangGraph with interrupts. Solo — every commit is mine; published to PyPI with 11 GitHub stars.
jobhunt.highlightTitle: My contribution — solo, shipped, and honest
jobhunt.highlightDesc: I built and shipped this solo: a deepagents/LangGraph agent that goes from raw CVs to approved applications with human-in-the-loop interrupts, graceful provider and data-source fallbacks, and a strict anti-hallucination rule. Published to PyPI and earning 11 GitHub stars.

c1.title: Full use of deepagents primitives
c1.desc: Subagents, a FilesystemBackend, interrupt_on plus a checkpointer for human-in-the-loop approval, and planning — the agent leans on the framework's primitives end to end rather than reimplementing them.

c2.title: Graceful degradation
c2.desc: Job sourcing falls back JSearch API → JobSpy scraper → bundled sample postings so it never hard-fails; the model provider chain degrades Anthropic → OpenRouter → OpenAI the same way.

c3.title: Persistent career memory
c3.desc: A career memory and application log persist across sessions so the agent never re-suggests a job it already proposed; per-call token and cost tracking is written to usage.jsonl.

c4.title: Anti-hallucination rule
c4.desc: Generated CVs and cover letters may only use facts drawn from the extracted profile — nothing is invented about the candidate.

c5.title: Shipped to PyPI
c5.desc: Published to PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publishing; 11 GitHub stars reflect real, unsolicited user interest.

openeval.title: OpenEval — LLM-as-Judge Evaluation
openeval.desc: A lightweight LLM-as-judge evaluation tool. It scores question/answer datasets across five dimensions — faithfulness, relevance, clarity, safety, and consistency — with a judge model, and compares two runs to show before/after deltas. Works with OpenAI, OpenRouter, or a local Ollama model. Solo — published to PyPI as openeval-llm (import and command name openeval).
openeval.highlightTitle: My contribution — a robust, shippable eval tool
openeval.highlightDesc: I designed and shipped OpenEval solo: a provider-agnostic LLM-as-judge CLI with a crash-proof judge, run-to-run delta comparison, honest cost accounting, and 12 tests. Published to PyPI as openeval-llm.

c1.title: run / compare CLI
c1.desc: A JSONL dataset goes in and a scored report comes out; compare prints a before/after delta table (e.g. faithfulness 0.71 → 0.88) so quality changes are visible at a glance.

c2.title: Crash-proof judge
c2.desc: Extracts JSON even when the model wraps it in fences or preamble, falls back to neutral defaults on missing or malformed dimensions, and retries transient errors — one bad answer can't take down a whole run.

c3.title: Report provenance & cost
c3.desc: Every report records the judge model, dataset, and timestamp; local Ollama models are billed at $0 so cost accounting stays honest.

c4.title: Pluggable connectors
c4.desc: The judge is decoupled from the subject under test, so you can grade a weak system with a stronger judge, and swap providers in a single line.

c5.title: Shipped to PyPI
c5.desc: PyPI trusted publishing, 12 tests, and English docs (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md).

finance.title: Personal Finance Analytics
finance.desc: An end-to-end personal proof of an enterprise analytics architecture: mock bank data → PostgreSQL FDW → dbt (staging / intermediate / marts / ai_layer) → a grounded AI insight engine → Superset. The AI never sees raw rows — dbt pre-aggregates each user into a one-row mart_ai_context, the single contract between the pipeline and the AI. Solo — built on a feature-branch + PR workflow.
finance.highlightTitle: My contribution — grounded AI on a real pipeline
finance.highlightDesc: I built this solo to prove an enterprise pattern end to end: the AI never touches raw rows — it reads a single pre-aggregated mart_ai_context contract, runs on versioned prompt templates with Pydantic-validated output, and is fully traced with OpenTelemetry.

c1.title: Grounding architecture
c1.desc: A mart_ai_context pre-aggregation feeds versioned Jinja2 prompt templates; Claude's structured output is validated with Pydantic, so every insight is reproducible and auditable.

c2.title: Layered dbt pipeline
c2.desc: staging → intermediate → marts → ai_layer on deterministic, seeded synthetic data, with dbt tests wired in as a CI gate.

c3.title: Full observability
c3.desc: OpenTelemetry exports to Tempo / Prometheus / Grafana, so a failed nightly run is a traced event rather than a silent gap.

c4.title: Two AI trigger modes
c4.desc: Insights run after each dbt build and on a schedule for anomaly and recommendation passes.

c5.title: Engineering discipline
c5.desc: A feature-branch + PR workflow even when working solo, with a README written as an interview-style Q&A.

analytics.title: Analytics Platform
analytics.sub: Qkare · Nov 2025 – May 2026 · Multi-tenant HR Analytics Stack
analytics.desc: End-to-end analytics platform for talent management — ingesting complex behavioral and cognitive data via PostgreSQL FDW, transforming it through a 3-layer dbt pipeline, and serving embedded Superset dashboards with per-tenant, per-user Row-Level Security. Primary owner of the dbt transformation layer, responsible for modeling, security, and the live delivery path.
analytics.highlightDesc: My core responsibility was turning fragmented assessment data into a production-grade decision layer. I standardized AON, Saville, and Assessment Center scores, built the mart structure, enforced tenant isolation with native RLS, and stabilized the end-to-end dashboard flow. Platform scaffolding (K8s, CI, guest-token API) was built in parallel by the team; my focus was data modeling, security, and delivery consistency.

c1.title: Problem
c1.desc: The problem was fragmentation: assessment data lived in separate systems, and non-technical teams could not reliably turn it into fast, trustworthy insights. Without a single decision layer, reporting and interpretation stayed inconsistent.

c2.title: My contribution
c2.desc: I consolidated the data into one analytics backbone: the dbt transformation layer, canonical score normalization, AI precompute marts, and reusable fact/seed structures for reporting. That made the same dataset consistent across both analysis and dashboarding.

c3.title: Impact
c3.desc: Native RLS made tenant isolation reliable and enabled secure reporting at 100+ model scale. It removed the dependency issues that were blocking production and directly unlocked live dashboard usage.

c4.title: Solution
c4.desc: I packaged the FDW → dbt → Superset loop so it could run with a single command: local DB provisioning, quick-start scripts, schema comparison tools, and mock-source integration to speed up development.

c5.title: Multi-tenant API & UAT deploy
c5.desc: Led the multi-tenancy refactor, UAT analytics-api configuration & Dockerfile, tenant-level GitLab CI security tests, and RLS alignment between the API and dbt layers.

c6.title: Production unblock & TR localisation
c6.desc: Fixed schema drift (candidate_user) blocking 130+ downstream models before a live demo; stabilised Superset Docker startup; updated Turkish UI translations and filter localisation (tr-TR).

coach.title: Coach Platform
coach.sub: Qkare · Sep 2025 – Nov 2025 · Multi-Agent AI Coaching
coach.desc: B2B corporate AI coaching platform — multi-agent orchestration with LangGraph, modular POML prompts, Parquet-grounded personalization, real-time voice via OpenAI Realtime API, and HR analytics dashboards. I owned the evaluation framework, the early voice stack, and the POML foundation.
coach.highlightTitle: My contribution — evaluation and voice layer
coach.highlightDesc: My main task in this project was to make the coach testable and measurable before production rollout. I designed and built the pattern engine and the Referee Agent for session quality scoring, shipped the first OpenAI Realtime voice path, and established the POML prompt structure. The pattern system was later handed off to and migrated by the team; analytics and performance-data integration also moved in parallel. My focus was quality assurance and interaction plumbing.

c1.title: Problem
c1.desc: The problem was that coaching flows depended too much on manual review, and neither the voice experience nor the prompt structure could be trusted before production. That made product behavior difficult to measure.

c2.title: My contribution
c2.desc: I built the LLM-as-Judge pipeline with the Referee Agent, combining the AI Client Simulator, multi-turn Coach integration, and the 5-dimension evaluation structure. I also made the POML prompt architecture and the voice path work together.

c3.title: Impact
c3.desc: The evaluation automation made session quality measurable, and the client simulator produced shorter, more consistent test flows. The real-time voice layer also turned the coach from a text-only tool into an interactive product.

c4.title: Solution
c4.desc: I designed and built the pattern engine, POML migration, voice bridge, and memory persistence as modular components. The pattern system was later handed off to the team and migrated; my lasting focus stayed on the evaluation and voice layers.

c5.title: Client simulator & eval tooling
c5.desc: Improved AI client simulator with turn-based progression guides and anti-repetition rules (49% fewer turns, score 7.88→8.2); added coaching evaluation reports and single-scenario test scripts.

c6.title: Contributor onboarding
c6.desc: Wrote comprehensive setup guide for new contributors (submodule init, Python 3.13+, dependency fixes) and expanded Referee Agent README with scenarios, token costs, and result JSON examples.

analyzer.title: Analyzer Platform
analyzer.sub: Qkare · Apr 2025 – May 2026 · Behavioral Analytics AI Agent
analyzer.desc: Corporate behavioral analytics platform — a LangChain ReAct agent that answers natural-language business intelligence queries over domain-specific behavioral data (Saville, 360°, 9-box, competency profiles) via dbt mart tables with native PostgreSQL RLS. Dual-service architecture: Analyzer (analysis engine) + Analyzer API (voice/text conversation layer). Lead engineer on security architecture, config bootstrap, and service integration.
analyzer.highlightDesc: Primary engineer on the security and configuration foundation that made production deployment possible: merged JWT + RLS + guard pipeline into main, built the bootstrap config system agents run on, and wired observability across both analyzer engine and conversation API. ReAct agent skills, company analytics chat, and enum refactors were developed in parallel by the team; my focus was tenant-safe data access, prompt architecture, and making the dual-service stack production-ready.

c1.title: 4-layer security & RLS pipeline
c1.desc: Shipped the full RLS-security branch: JWT auth on HTTP + WebSocket, UserContext via ContextVar, execute_rls on all SQL paths, InputGuard/OutputGuard with YAML-driven threat patterns, cross-tenant isolation guard, and QueryValidationPipeline before every LLM call.

c2.title: Config bootstrap & schema layer
c2.desc: Introduced backend/core/bootstrap/ (ConfigPathResolver, agent_config, mod_loaders); consolidated application/schema; moved position_models and security types; eliminated duplicate config packages and hardcoded prompt strings.

c3.title: Master prompt & agent reliability
c3.desc: Fixed master prompt configurations rendering (dict sections → block strings), build_master_prompt() guards, PostgreSQL TCP keepalive + stale-connection retry, SQL alias ambiguity fixes, and chat history trimming for context overflow.

c4.title: Domain mods & analysis fixes
c4.desc: Fixed FourHProfileRiskMod runtime TypeError, resolved module initialization circular import via PEP 562 lazy loading, unified SwotPatternType enums, and enhanced ProfileTransformer for employee_user_id identity validation.

c5.title: Voice layer integration
c5.desc: Langfuse trace propagation across analyzer client and chat API caller; fail-fast startup validation for analyzer service URL; global 500 error logging; improved employee ID extraction from nested survey payloads.

c6.title: Langfuse tracing
c6.desc: Integrated Langfuse callbacks in AnalizciAgent and WebSocket endpoints for end-to-end LLM trace propagation; docker-compose config and utility module for observability across the analysis pipeline.

aisvc.title: AI Microservices — KPI & Skills Advisor
aisvc.sub: Qkare · Aug 2025 – May 2026 · KPI & competency recommendation microservices
aisvc.desc: Two production B2B AI microservices behind the corporate agent gateway. The KPI Advisor recommends SMART-aligned KPIs for any position, grounded in the APQC Process Classification Framework (lexical + category-prior retrieval, no vector DB) and validated by an offline LLM-as-judge pipeline. The Skills Advisor selects exactly 12 competency dimensions from a closed dictionary for any job title, with Turkish rationales and a DSPy 5-expert ensemble. Primary engineer on both — generation engines, retrieval, the eval flywheel, caching, and production integration.
aisvc.highlightTitle: My contribution — two recommendation engines, end to end
aisvc.highlightDesc: Primary engineer on both microservices, from the founding competency UI and the APQC-grounded KPI generator through DSPy consensus, embedding-free retrieval, multi-layer caching, and the offline eval flywheel. Observability, UI polish, and O*NET / LangChain-v1 migrations were developed in parallel by the team; my focus was structured-output quality, constrained generation, and shipping both behind one gateway contract.

c1.title: KPI generation service & APQC retrieval
c1.desc: Built the core FastAPI service: OpenAI JSON-mode structured output, hybrid lexical + category-prior retrieval over 1631 PCF nodes and 2680 metrics, diversity selection, confidence-aware fallback, and prompt assembly with a domain-expert persona and corporate rules.

c2.title: Offline quality flywheel
c2.desc: Implemented the step2 LLM-as-judge eval (SMART ×0.35 + Relevance ×0.40 + Clarity ×0.25), step3 Excel export for human review, and step4 feedback import into the golden dataset — closing the synthetic-data → eval → human flywheel.

c3.title: Founding competency assessment system
c3.desc: Built the founding AI competency-suggestion flow: Ocean/Saville test types, dimension scoring with Turkish rationales, and a Streamlit two-column UI — establishing the product before the modular FastAPI refactor.

c4.title: Closed-dictionary constrained generation
c4.desc: Moved dimensions and mappings into a ~900-line dimensions.json with /dimensions endpoints; per-request shuffle to reduce positional bias; whitelist validation, dedupe, and a hard cap of 12 on every response.

c5.title: DSPy SME expert ensemble
c5.desc: A dynamic SME weight system with an arithmetic-mean adjudicator: five parallel expert personas merge into a consensus list with category balance — an alternative to single-pass LangChain for higher-stakes roles.

c6.title: Caching & production integration
c6.desc: SHA-256 LLM-level and request-level caches so identical requests skip the model entirely; JWT + tenant whitelist via grid-auth; and gateway invoke wiring (/api/v1/agents/…/invoke) for downstream HR workflows.

gateway.title: Agent API Gateway
gateway.sub: Qkare · Apr 2026 – May 2026 · Multi-agent AI API Monorepo
gateway.desc: Corporate AI agent gateway — a FastAPI monorepo that exposes multiple downstream AI microservices through one standard invoke API: shared JWT auth, request/response envelope, structured logging, and OpenTelemetry hooks. I led platform bootstrap, auth integration, Docker topology, dynamic CI, and the Cookiecutter-based agent scaffold.
gateway.highlightTitle: My contribution — platform contract and deployment
gateway.highlightDesc: My main task was to make the shared entry point for the agent ecosystem production-ready. I built the monorepo layout, first agent wiring, JWT auth, per-agent Docker/compose pattern, dynamic CI generation, and the Cookiecutter scaffold for new agents. The rest of the team iterated on create_gateway_app, CORS, and scaffolding improvements in parallel; my focus was the contract and deployment topology.

c1.title: Problem
c1.desc: The problem was that each agent integration created repeated boilerplate and rising maintenance cost without a shared auth and deployment pattern. New services needed a common entry point, plus a Cookiecutter scaffold they could start from.

c2.title: My contribution
c2.desc: I built the shared gateway core: router factory, invoke schemas, JWT verification, per-agent container structure, and dynamic CI generation. That made the base scaffolding manageable from one place.

c3.title: Impact
c3.desc: The gateway reduced integration cost by unifying different AI services behind one standard invoke flow. With the container and auth contract clear, teams could ship agents faster.

c4.title: Solution
c4.desc: I designed per-agent Docker Compose, dynamic GitLab child pipelines, and one invoke contract. That turned the gateway into a low-friction platform for new services.

c5.title: First production agent integration
c5.desc: Wired ga-kpi-advisor as the reference agent: httpx proxy to downstream KPI service, env-based URL config, validation error logging, and end-to-end invoke flow from Streamlit through the gateway.

c6.title: Cross-service contract alignment
c6.desc: Kept gateway payloads in sync with KPI Advisor schema evolution: leadershipLevel, positionGroup, name/definition fields, optional id — ensuring monorepo invoke examples match the downstream Pydantic models.

thesis.title: Hybrid ABC-PSO — Payment-Preference Classification
thesis.sub: Academic · MSc Thesis · metaheuristic ML optimization
thesis.desc: My MSc thesis: classifying digital payment-method preferences on the Fed DCPC 2024 dataset. I implemented a Hybrid ABC-PSO swarm optimizer from the paper — no black-box library — to tune Random Forest hyperparameters, validated it on standard benchmark functions, and explained the model with SHAP. Solo — end to end, from data cleaning to a reproducible pipeline and an honest, problem-dependent result.

c1.title: Hybrid ABC-PSO implemented by hand
c1.desc: Implemented the hybrid metaheuristic from the paper without a black-box library: sine-cosine velocity update, dynamic inertia weight, an ABC crossover phase, and a fitness cache to avoid recomputing expensive cross-validation scores.

c2.title: Feature engineering & encoding
c2.desc: IQR + z-score outlier suppression, temporal features such as payday period, and hybrid categorical encoding (one-hot for low cardinality, target encoding for high) with SelectFromModel feature selection.

c3.title: Honest, rigorous evaluation
c3.desc: Chose balanced accuracy for the imbalanced multi-class problem, used stratified cross-validation, and reported honestly that the hybrid doesn't always win — it's problem-dependent. Independently validated the optimizer on Sphere / Rastrigin benchmark functions.

c4.title: SHAP explanations
c4.desc: Global and local SHAP explanations for the tuned Random Forest, so the model's decisions stay interpretable rather than a black box.

thesis.highlightTitle: My contribution — research-grade ML, end to end
thesis.highlightDesc: Sole author from data to results: I built the hybrid metaheuristic myself, engineered the features, and reported findings honestly rather than cherry-picking. A full TR + EN thesis, an IJISRT paper, and defense slides back it up.
